questions.question: Which sentence is in the Present Continuous tense?
questions.explanation: Present Continuous uses 'is/am/are' + verb-ing to show an action happening right now.

misconceptions.reason: Student confuses Simple Present (habit) with Present Continuous (now)

steps: Imagine you do something every day. 'I eat lunch at 1 PM.' That's Simple Present.
Now imagine you are DOING it right now. 'I AM EAT-ING lunch.' That's Present Continuous.
Look for the '-ing' and the helping verb (am/is/are)!
Simple = Habit. Continuous = Action in progress.
story.moral: Use '-ing' for things happening right now!

questions.question: Choose the correct past tense form: 'She ____ to the market yesterday.'
questions.explanation: 'Went' is the irregular past tense form of 'go'.

misconceptions.reason: Student over-regularizes by adding '-ed' to an irregular verb

steps: You know that usually we add '-ed' to make past tense (like 'walk' → 'walked').
But some verbs are REBELS! They don't follow the rules.
'Go' is a rebel. It changes completely to 'WENT'.
There is no such word as 'goed' in English!
story.moral: Watch out for rebel verbs that don't follow the '-ed' rule!

questions.question: What is the future tense of 'read'?
questions.explanation: To form the future tense, we add 'will' before the base form of the verb.

misconceptions.reason: Student uses past tense instead of future

steps: Think about when the action happens.
Yesterday = Read (pronounced 'red').
Tomorrow = WILL read.
Future always needs a helper like 'WILL' or 'GOING TO'.
story.moral: Future needs a helper! Always use 'will' for future actions.

questions.question: Which of these is a 'helping verb'?

misconceptions.reason: Student confuses main action verbs with helping verbs

steps: A Main Verb is like a STAR - it shows the action (Run, Jump, Eat).
A Helping Verb is like an ASSISTANT - it helps the star (Is running, Has eaten).
Run is the action star. 'Has' is the assistant helping to show time.
Helping verbs don't show much action on their own.
story.moral: Helping verbs assist the main action verb.

questions.question: Choose the correct verb: 'The team ____ playing very well today.'
questions.explanation: Collective nouns like 'team' are usually treated as singular in this context, so we use 'is'.

misconceptions.reason: Student thinks because a team has many people, it must be plural

steps: Imagine 11 players. That's many people.
But they are all in ONE group called a 'Team'.
Think of the team as a single BOX containing the players.
There is only ONE box, so we say 'The box IS...', not 'The box ARE...'.
Same for 'team' - it's one unit!
story.moral: Treat a collective group as one single unit (singular).
